mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate over the prior three months reduces discount rates and eases financial conditions, lifting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if 20d forward SPX returns are not higher after 3-month FEDFUNDS declines than after flat or rising FEDFUNDS in at least 60% of rolling 2-year windows, or if rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above 4.5% signals weakening labor demand and recession risk, causing equity earnings expectations to fall and S&P 500 forward returns to be negative over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if episodes where UNRATE crosses above 4.5% are followed by positive 60d SPX returns more than 55% of the time, or if the signal loses sign consistency in a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When RSAFS and PAYEMS both accelerate, household spending and payroll income are expanding together, which supports revenue growth and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition of rising RSAFS and PAYEMS does not outperform either series alone, or if triggered periods fail to show positive excess 20d SPX returns relative to unconditional baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in INDPRO indicates stronger industrial output and improving profit-sensitive cyclicals, which should lift S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if positive monthly or 3-month changes in INDPRO are not associated with higher 5d SPX returns than negative changes, or if the effect disappears after controlling for broad market trend.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly drop in HOUST below a 6-month moving average signals weakening housing demand and construction activity, which leads S&P 500 returns to be negative over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls below its 6-month moving average by more than 5%
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST breakdowns are followed by positive 20d SPX returns more than 60% of the time, or if the trigger has no incremental predictive power versus a no-trigger baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation is falling while FEDFUNDS remains high, real policy becomes less restrictive and equity multiples expand, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the combination of falling CPIAUCSL and elevated FEDFUNDS does not outperform either series alone, or if 60d SPX returns are not positive in at least 55% of such episodes.

mechanism_narrative: In a soft-landing regime, a declining UNRATE that coincides with stable PAYEMS indicates improving employment quality without recession, which supports risk appetite and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the paired condition of falling UNRATE and stable or rising PAYEMS does not produce above-baseline 20d SPX returns, or if the sign reverses outside the stated regime.

mechanism_narrative: When GDP growth is positive but retail sales and housing both decelerate, the expansion is losing breadth and earnings risk rises, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if positive GDPC1 with weakening RSAFS and HOUST does not predict negative 60d SPX returns, or if the interaction term fails to add value beyond GDP growth alone.
